title.en: Flask Forms & Database: Handling User Input
title.ta: Flask படிவங்கள் & தரவுத்தளம்: பயனர் உள்ளீட்டை கையாளுதல்

description.en: Learn to handle forms with WTForms, validate user input, and store data with SQLAlchemy ORM.
description.ta: WTForms மூலம் படிவங்களைக் கையாளவும், பயனர் உள்ளீட்டை சரிபார்க்கவும், SQLAlchemy ORM மூலம் தரவைச் சேமிக்கவும் கற்றுக்கொள்ளுங்கள்.

story_variants.en:
## The Registration Desk

Vaathiyaar sets up a registration booth. "Every web app needs to collect data from users — names, emails, passwords. But you can't just trust whatever they type! You need **forms** to structure the input and **validation** to check it. Then you need a **database** to store it."

### Flask-WTF: Form Handling
- **WTForms** provides form classes with fields and validators
- **CSRF protection** prevents cross-site request forgery
- **Validation** ensures data meets your rules before processing

### SQLAlchemy: The ORM
- **ORM** = Object-Relational Mapping
- Write Python classes instead of raw SQL
- Each class = a database table
- Each instance = a row in the table

### The Flow

```
User fills form → Flask validates → SQLAlchemy saves → Database stores
     ↑                                                      |
     └──────────── Show success/error page ←────────────────┘
```

> **Key Concept:** Never trust user input. Always validate on the server side, even if you validate on the client side too. WTForms makes this easy with built-in validators like DataRequired, Email, Length, and EqualTo.

steps.line: from flask import Flask, render_template, redirect, url_for, flash

explanation.en: Import Flask helpers including flash for user messages
Import SQLAlchemy for database operations
Import FlaskForm as the base class for all forms

steps.line: from wtforms import StringField, IntegerField, SubmitField

explanation.en: Import field types — each maps to an HTML input element

steps.line: from wtforms.validators import DataRequired, Length, NumberRange

explanation.en: Import validators to enforce rules on form fields
Create the Flask application
Required for CSRF protection and session management
Configure SQLite database — the file will be created automatically
Initialize SQLAlchemy with our Flask app
Define a Student model — this creates a 'student' table in the database
Auto-incrementing primary key for each student
Name field — max 100 chars, cannot be empty
Age field — stored as an integer
Email must be unique — no two students can share an email
Define a form class that inherits from FlaskForm
Name field with required + length validators
Age field must be a number between 10 and 120
The submit button for the form
Route handles GET (show form) and POST (process form)
validate_on_submit() checks if it's POST AND all validators pass

animation_sequence.title: Form Validation and Database Save
animation_sequence.code:
from flask_wtf import FlaskForm
from wtforms import StringField
from wtforms.validators import DataRequired, Length

class StudentForm(FlaskForm):
    name = StringField('Name', validators=[DataRequired(), Length(min=2, max=100)])

form = StudentForm(data={'name': 'Alice'})
is_valid = form.validate()
print(f'Valid: {is_valid}')
print(f'Errors: {form.errors}')

executionSteps.explanation: Import FlaskForm base class for form definitions
Import StringField for text input fields
Define form class -- each attribute becomes an HTML form field
validate() runs all validators -- 'Alice' passes DataRequired and Length
Form is valid, safe to save to database

instruction.en: Create a Flask app with a SQLAlchemy model called 'Book' with fields: title (string), author (string), pages (integer). Write a route '/add' that accepts POST data as JSON to add a book, and '/books' that returns all books as JSON.
instruction.ta: title, author, pages புலங்களுடன் 'Book' என்ற SQLAlchemy மாதிரியுடன் Flask பயன்பாட்டை உருவாக்குங்கள். புத்தகத்தைச் சேர்க்க JSON ஏற்கும் '/add' வழியும், அனைத்து புத்தகங்களையும் JSON ஆகத் திருப்பும் '/books' வழியும் எழுதுங்கள்.

practice_challenges.starter_code:
from flask import Flask, request, jsonify
from flask_sqlalchemy import SQLAlchemy

app = Flask(__name__)
app.config['SQLALCHEMY_DATABASE_URI'] = 'sqlite:///books.db'
db = SQLAlchemy(app)

class Book(db.Model):
    id = db.Column(db.Integer, primary_key=True)
    title = db.Column(db.String(200), nullable=False)
    author = db.Column(db.String(100), nullable=False)
    pages = db.Column(db.Integer, nullable=False)

@app.route('/add', methods=['POST'])
def add_book():
    data = request.get_json()
    book = Book(title=data['title'], author=data['author'], pages=data['pages'])
    db.session.add(book)
    db.session.commit()
    return jsonify({'message': f'Added: {book.title}'}), 201

@app.route('/books')
def get_books():
    books = Book.query.all()
    return jsonify([{'id': b.id, 'title': b.title, 'author': b.author, 'pages': b.pages} for b in books])

if __name__ == '__main__':
    with app.app_context():
        db.create_all()
    app.run(debug=True)

en: Define your model class inheriting from db.Model
Use db.Column() to define each field
db.session.add() stages the record, db.session.commit() saves it
Book.query.all() returns all rows as Python objects
ta: Book.query.all() அனைத்து வரிசைகளையும் Python பொருள்களாகத் திருப்புகிறது

q.en: What does validate_on_submit() check in Flask-WTF?

en: Only if the form was submitted
If it's a POST request AND all validators pass
If the database connection is active
ta: POST கோரிக்கையா மற்றும் அனைத்து சரிபார்ப்பாளர்களும் தேர்ச்சி பெற்றதா

q.en: In SQLAlchemy, what does db.session.commit() do?

en: Saves all pending changes to the database
ta: நிலுவையில் உள்ள அனைத்து மாற்றங்களையும் தரவுத்தளத்தில் சேமிக்கிறது

q.en: Why is SECRET_KEY required in Flask-WTF forms?
